task_completion.description: Verify the promotion name and store assignment
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Name' field containing 'Volume Discount - Buy 5+ Save 10%' and the 'Stores' field with 'Urban Electronics' selected or checked in the Drupal Commerce promotion form.

task_completion.description: Verify the offer type and discount percentage
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Offer type' dropdown set to 'Order item percentage off' and the corresponding percentage field set to '10' (or '0.10').

task_completion.description: Verify the condition for order item quantity
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Conditions' section showing 'Order item quantity' selected, an operator dropdown set to '>=' (or 'greater than or equal to'), and a quantity field containing '5'.

task_completion.description: Verify the promotion is active and automatic (no coupons)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Active' checkbox being checked. Ensure there are no coupons generated or required (the promotion should apply automatically).

task_completion.description: Verify the promotion was successfully saved
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a green success message stating the promotion has been saved, or verify the promotion appears in the active promotions list at /admin/commerce/promotions.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web GUI to create the promotion
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Drupal Commerce web interface in a browser, not terminal commands like drush or direct database queries.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a real web browser rendering the Drupal admin interface, not a text editor modifying configuration files.

integrity.description: Verify results are not faked using browser developer tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The success messages and form values should appear naturally rendered without signs of DOM manipulation or open developer console panels.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The promotion must be named 'Volume Discount - Buy 5+ Save 10%' and assigned to the 'Urban Electronics' store. The Offer type must be 'Order item percentage off' with a value of 10 (or 0.10 internally). The Condition must be 'Order item quantity' with the operator '>=' (greater than or equal to) and a quantity of 5. The promotion must be active and not require coupons (automatic).
